entry.title: Washington Post: 12 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Israel infighting could strip legal protections from West Bank settlers
  • In Kherson, misery under Russian occupation, hope over Ukrainian gains
  • Russia likely to seize all of Luhansk in coming weeks, U.S. official says
  • Russia controls most of Severodonetsk, governor says
  • Bolivia sentences ex-president to 10 years in prison after coup trial
  • Biden says Zelensky ‘didn’t want to hear’ U.S. warnings of invasion
  • Haiti’s gangs use TikTok, Instagram, Twitter to recruit and terrorize
  • Amid Ukraine war, husband and wife doctor team runs front-line hospital
  • Venezuelan opposition leader attacked during national tour
  • Pair's disappearance in Brazil's Amazon tied to 'fish mafia'
  • Mexico issues 7,000 temporary documents to migrants in south
  • UN says concerned over Libya clashes, urges efforts for calm
  • Man killed in floods in Turkey's capital
  • Venezuelan leader, Iranian president sign 20-year agreement
  • Nicaragua authorizes entry of Russian troops, planes, ships
  • War, guilt and last kisses: A deceptive, uneasy calm in Kyiv
  • Germany to introduce some border checks during G7 summit
  • Survivor of abusive facility searches for lost Korean roots
  • 31 tied to hate group charged with planning riot near Idaho Pride event
  • The town crier
  • March for Our Lives 2022: Thousands gather to protest gun violence
  • Tom Rice tries to survive his Trump impeachment vote — and stay in the House
  • She tracked her boyfriend using an AirTag — then killed him, police say
  • The shadow race is underway for the Republican presidential nomination
  • Man arrested for leaving flowers on fiancee’s grave found guilty of littering
  • Judge: Jan. 6 hearings may help defendants shift accountability to Trump
  • The Google engineer who thinks the company’s AI has come to life
  • Silicon Valley braces for tech pullback after a decade of decadence
  • Australia’s Early Flu Season Shows Americans Need Their Shots
  • US Hurricane Season Will Be Felt Around the World This Year
  • Behind the Nickel Mess on the London Metal Exchange
  • Zelensky calls for international support for Taiwan before China attacks
  • In Russia, a Christian religion is punished, over and over again
  • The Post endorses Brooke Lierman in Democratic primary for Maryland comptroller
  • Supreme Court justices should be able to feel safe at home
  • Ask Eugene Robinson about the latest news around the U.S. and world
  • Chat with Perry Bacon Jr. about politics and beyond
  • Chat with Jennifer Rubin about her columns, politics, policy and more
  • Send a letter to the editor
  • Contact the reader representative
  • Submit an op-ed
Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: Guardian: 12 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Russia-Ukraine war: fierce fighting in Sievierdonetsk, as EU to decide on Ukraine candidacy – live
  • Film bans are less about offence, more ‘community leaders’ showing who’s boss | Kenan Malik
  • Chris Patten: ‘We have a populist government that is – fatally – not popular’

  • Kristen Stewart’s gay ghost hunt is the spookiest of Pride tie-ins | Rebecca Nicholson

  • Unfinished work by Leonardo da Vinci heads ‘home’ to French chateau where he died
  • Vieux Farka Touré: Les Racines review – exquisite return to the Malian source
  • Teenage clicks – a century of British youth culture caught on camera
  • Rocking the Scandi style
  • On my radar: Emily Maitlis’s cultural highlights
  • Freddie Mercury’s ‘priceless’ stamp collection to be celebrated
  • Found: the ‘how to draw’ books Pablo Picasso created for his daughter
  • Blacklist Dubai over failure to crack down on Russian oligarchs, say campaigners
  • Ivanka turns on daddy but she’s just a Trump looking out for No 1 as usual | Arwa Mahdawi
  • Billie Eilish review – louder than ever
  • Pete Doherty on Kate Moss: ‘Our relationship became a running battle: highs and then crushing, violent lows’
  • Forget the thrill of the chase, only rarely do football’s biggest signings work out | Jonathan Wilson
  • ‘I’m not afraid of clowns’: Republican defends vote to impeach Trump
  • Zelenskiy ‘didn’t want to hear’ warnings of Russia invasion, says Biden

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 12 Jun, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 12 Jun, 2022 published at 2022-06-12 06:34AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Hong Kong police charge four suspects in connection with shooting during gang skirmish in Central
  • Unesco-awarded Tai O Heritage Hotel in the ‘Venice of Hong Kong’ unveils facelift in push for eco-tourism and community ties
  • Will Beijing resume dialogue with Hong Kong’s opposition camp? Ex-Legco president Jasper Tsang says this will signal success of ‘one country, two systems’
  • In a Hong Kong led by an ex-police officer, elite administrative officers will still play a major role
  • Hong Kong FCC axed human rights press awards to avoid risk of jail, chief cites concerns about independence of courts under national security law
  • DAB vice-chairman Horace Cheung likely to be picked as Hong Kong’s first deputy secretary for justice
  • Almost 40 per cent of Hong Kong’s outsourced government workers unaware of labour rights, survey finds
  • Hong Kong’s finance chief warns against suspected phishing scam after his picture appears in online adverts
  • Hong Kong’s chief justice stresses importance of senior counsel, as 5 barristers appointed to prestigious rank
  • Hong Kong has proposed banning CBD products within a year. Here’s everything you need to know
  • Hong Kong homebuyers snap up Sun Hung Kai’s Silicon Hill flats for the second weekend in a row, aiming to get ahead of higher interest rates
  • China Covid-19 cases rise, as Beijing bar cluster and Inner Mongolia trigger worries
  • China’s Africa affairs chief to visit debt-hit Zambia days after Xi Jinping call with president
  • We will fight to the very end, Chinese defence chief warns on Taiwan independence
  • Why China is likely to focus more on Central Asia as US rivalry intensifies
  • Chinese defence officials take aim at US’ ‘aggressive’ Taiwan-Ukraine comparison
  • Covid cases bounce back in Taiwan after week of declines
  • ‘Asian water tower’ is facing a worsening supply imbalance, study finds
  • Man in China who stole US$3,700 from a classmate on his wedding day, then ‘loaned’ back some stolen cash to groom gets 2 years jail
  • Nine held in Chinese city after fierce attack on women diners sends two to hospital
  • Can vaccination or drugs help defeat long Covid?
  • Why Virat Kohli, Anushka Sharma, Genelia D’Souza and other Indian A-listers are endorsing plant-based meat
  • Shangri-La Dialogue: Seoul to ‘dramatically’ enhance defence, work with Japan to deter North Korea, defence minister says
  • Sri Lanka PM Ranil Wickremesinghe open to buying Russian oil, accepting China’s financial help
  • Will Indonesia’s quality tourism plan push out domestic visitors from Borobudur?
  • What’s New Zealand’s plan as superpower rivalry escalates in the Pacific?
  • Are Indonesia, Malaysia and Sri Lanka really ‘victims’ of China’s belt and road ‘debt trap diplomacy’?
  • Asean should have a ‘full-time’ Myanmar special envoy with added powers, Malaysia’s Saifuddin Abdullah says
  • China-Australia relations: Canberra says it values China trade ties, urges Beijing to be ‘transparent’ about military build-up
  • Ukraine war: Kyiv officials dispute Joe Biden’s claim Zelensky ‘didn’t want to hear’ warnings that Putin would invade
  • President Emmanuel Macron’s absolute majority at risk as France elects new parliament
  • Canada, in a world first, proposes health warnings on every cigarette in bid to curb smoking
  • Ukraine war: Russia said to be using more deadly weapons during invasion
  • Oklahoma attorney general seeks execution dates for 25 death row inmates
  • In Kyiv, EU chief promises a signal on Ukraine’s candidate status bid next week
  • Spain’s Catholics want Rome to consider optional celibacy and women priests
  • On New Mexico visit, Joe Biden faces residents’ fury over government efforts to tackle fires
  • Newly discovered dinosaur was a ‘huge’ 32-ft-long predator with a crocodile face, say scientists
  • Thousands of protesters stream to Washington’s National Mall to demand gun law changes
  • Sanctions-hit Iran signs 20-year cooperation deal with Venezuela; allies vow to resist Washington
  • US judge dismisses rape lawsuit against Cristiano Ronaldo in Las Vegas
  • Nicaragua authorises entry of Russian troops, planes and ships
  • Rescuers in Italy locate 7 bodies at scorched site of helicopter crash
  • Diplomacy, punitive sanctions only useful as ‘pre-emptive’ measures to stop wars, Zelensky says at Shangri-La Dialogue
  • The rise and fall of Secoo: how China’s top luxury retailer fell off the catwalk after glory days of US$140 million Nasdaq IPO
  • Samsung Asset Management launches first blockchain-focused ETF in Hong Kong as technology grows beyond cryptocurrencies
  • The ‘Asean way’ has more to offer Asia than a Nato-like security alliance
  • How pandemic-weary young people are embracing ‘live now, pay later’ finance solutions
  • Almost all of Chinese mainland on board with hydrogen development, as Beijing banks on clean fuel for carbon-neutrality drive
  • New Oriental’s Yu Minhong brings back English teaching with a live-streaming e-commerce twist
  • Melinda Zeman on her Hong Kong childhood, becoming an Orthodox Jew, and finding a creative outlet in jewellery design
  • Sir Robert Ho Tung, man and money: how a Hong Kong Eurasian built a myth and a fortune, and became the richest person in the territory
  • ‘Freedom swimmers’ from China to Hong Kong who braved deadly waters, and police officers assigned to catch and return them, recall dark days
  • How Bruce Lee’s Way of the Dragon made Chuck Norris a star with their fight in Rome’s Colosseum, and what the film showed about Lee’s directing
  • Don’t be like Johnny Depp and Amber Heard – the signs of a toxic relationship to look out for and when to walk away
  • The right swimsuit for you? It’s whatever you like regardless of your body type – low-cut or high-leg, bikini or one-piece
  • Ms Marvel directors hope Marvel’s first Muslim superhero series inspires ‘a lot of girls in the world’ as well as people of any religion, race or genders
  • Amid China’s civil war between Communists and Nationalists, how two sisters were separated by fate and kept apart through their successes
  • Breathwork, movement, sound and social interaction create ‘core steadiness’ you need to relieve stress and anxiety in the face of change, yoga therapist says
  • 6 surprising food facts about Queen Elizabeth, according to a royal chef – Darren McGrady formerly cooked for her and Princess Diana, and revealed her chocolate habit and spoiling of her corgis
  • Where are the original Iron Chefs now? Before Netflix rebooted the 1990s Japanese reality show that inspired Masterchef and Hell’s Kitchen, its star chefs opened restaurants worldwide
  • Inside Jeffree Star’s controversial US$200 million net worth: the YouTuber and vegan beauty mogul bought a yak farm, splurges on hot pink Lamborghinis, fuchsia Hermès Birkins, and owns mega mansions
  • David and Victoria Beckham’s multimillion-dollar property empire: from a stylish Miami penthouse and a country estate near Prince Harry and Meghan Markle, to a mansion in London and homes in Dubai
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.content:
Articles in this issue:

The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.

entry.title: Taipei Times: 12 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
